subject: [PATCH v4 2/4] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that since sv_nrpools is either '1' or equal to svc_pool_map.count, it can be removed and only sv_is_pooled used for simplification and clarity in the code.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this patch is good, but there is probably room for further
simplification which is probably best left to a later patch.
In particular, ->sv_nrpools is now either '1' or svc_pool_map.count,
and these possibilities match ->sv_is_pooled.  So we only need one of
those.
I think removing ->sv_nrpools and only using ->sv_is_pooled would
simplify/clarify some of the code.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to pernode, removing other modes such as SVC_POOL_AUTO/GLOBAL/PERCPU/PERNODE. The default behavior on multi-NUMA hosts changes from a single global pool to one pool per NUMA node. This change simplifies the code and improves performance by providing proper thread affinity and NUMA-local memory allocation. The patch also removes deprecated code, including mode selection heuristics and switch statements, and updates documentation to reflect the new behavior.
